name: The Salyersville National Bank
description_short: Community bank established in 1902 in Magoffin County, Kentucky, offering FDIC-insured deposit accounts and banking services with a local, personal touch.
description_long:
Salyersville National Bank was founded in 1902 and has operated as a small community bank in Magoffin County, eastern Kentucky for over 120 years. The institution is FDIC-insured, meaning deposits are backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government up to applicable limits. The bank positions itself as a locally rooted institution with deep community ties, emphasizing personal service and accessibility to both current and prospective customers.

The bank offers standard banking products and services typical of community banks, including transaction accounts (checking and savings), though specific product details are not fully enumerated on their accessible website pages. They emphasize quality service delivery and reasonable profitability as core operational principles. The bank maintains online banking capabilities and provides resources related to conducting secure transactions online and consumer protection information.

Salyersville National Bank distinguishes itself through its emphasis on community roots, local decision-making, and personalized service delivery. The bank's marketing tagline—"Home Grown & Strong"—reflects its identity as a regional institution embedded in eastern Kentucky culture. The bank explicitly welcomes both existing customers and those considering new accounts, positioning accessibility and relationship-building as central to their business model.

This is a legitimate, regulated financial institution with proper FDIC insurance and NMLS licensing (#402250). However, potential customers should note that detailed product information, rate schedules, and specific service offerings are not comprehensively available on the public website. Interested consumers should contact the bank directly for complete information about account types, fees, interest rates, and lending products. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Established 1902 — over 120 years of operational history and community presence
Licensed and regulated institution with NMLS number #402250
Emphasizes personal service and relationship banking model
Accessible to both existing customers and new account applicants
Provides online banking and secure transaction capabilities
Resources for consumer protection including scam prevention for seniors
cons: Limited detailed product information available on website — rates, fees, and specific offerings not transparent
Small community bank with limited geographic footprint; primarily serves eastern Kentucky
About page returns 404 error, reducing accessible company information
No information provided regarding lending products, credit cards, or loan rates
Minimal digital presence compared to regional or national banks
best_for: Residents of Magoffin County and eastern Kentucky seeking local banking relationships
Customers prioritizing personalized service and community-focused banking
Those seeking basic deposit accounts with FDIC insurance protection
diagnosis: Best for eastern Kentucky residents and community bank customers who value personal relationships and local decision-making over digital convenience or product breadth. Main caveat: comprehensive product details, rates, and fees are not publicly available online—direct contact with the bank is necessary to evaluate specific offerings and compare competitively.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
